name: mkdocs
documentation:
# MkDocs

> *Project documentation with Markdown*

[![PyPI Version][pypi-v-image]][pypi-v-link]
[![Build Status][GHAction-image]][GHAction-link]
[![Coverage Status][codecov-image]][codecov-link]

MkDocs is a **fast**, **simple** and **downright gorgeous** static site
generator that's geared towards building project documentation. Documentation
source files are written in Markdown, and configured with a single YAML
configuration file. It is designed to be easy to use and can be extended with
third-party themes, plugins, and Markdown extensions.

Please see the [Documentation][mkdocs] for an introductory tutorial and a full
user guide.

## Features

- Build static HTML files from Markdown files.
- Use Plugins and Markdown Extensions to enhance MkDocs.
- Use the built-in themes, third party themes or create your own.
- Publish your documentation anywhere that static files can be served.
- Much more!

## Support

If you need help with MkDocs, do not hesitate to get in contact with us!

-   For questions and high-level discussions, use **[Discussions]** on GitHub.
    -   For small questions, a good alternative is the **[Chat room]** on
        Gitter/Matrix.
-   To report a bug or make a feature request, open an **[Issue]** on GitHub.

Please note that we may only provide
support for problems/questions regarding core features of MkDocs. Any
questions or bug reports about features of third-party themes, plugins,
extensions or similar should be made to their respective projects.  
But, such questions are *not* banned from the [chat room].

Make sure to stick around to answer some questions as well!

## Links

- [Official Documentation][mkdocs]
- [Latest Release Notes][release-notes]
- [Catalog of third-party plugins, themes and recipes][catalog]

## Contributing to MkDocs

The MkDocs project welcomes, and depends on, contributions from developers and
users in the open source community. Please see the [Contributing Guide] for
information on how you can help.

## Code of Conduct

Everyone interacting in the MkDocs project's codebases, issue trackers, and
discussion forums is expected to follow the [PyPA Code of Conduct].

classes.name: mkdocs.exceptions.MkDocsException
documentation:
The base class which all MkDocs exceptions inherit from. This should
not be raised directly. One of the subclasses should be raised instead.

classes.name: mkdocs.exceptions.Abort
documentation: Abort the build.

classes.name: mkdocs.exceptions.ConfigurationError
documentation:
This error is raised by configuration validation when a validation error
is encountered. This error should be raised by any configuration options
defined in a plugin's [config_scheme][].

classes.name: mkdocs.exceptions.BuildError
documentation:
This error may be raised by MkDocs during the build process. Plugins should
not raise this error.

classes.name: mkdocs.exceptions.PluginError
documentation:
A subclass of [`mkdocs.exceptions.BuildError`][] which can be raised by plugin
events.

classes.name: mkdocs.theme.Theme

methods.name: mkdocs.theme.Theme._load_theme_config
documentation: Recursively load theme and any parent themes.

methods.name: mkdocs.theme.Theme.get_env
documentation: Return a Jinja environment for the theme.

A Theme object.

Args:
    name: The name of the theme as defined by its entrypoint.
    custom_dir: User defined directory for custom templates.
    static_templates: A list of templates to render as static pages.

All other keywords are passed as-is and made available as a key/value mapping.

functions.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.get_schema
documentation: Soft-deprecated, do not use.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig

methods.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.load_file
documentation: Load config options from the open file descriptor of a YAML file.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.config_file_path
documentation: The path to the mkdocs.yml config file. Can't be populated from the config.
type: ignore[assignment]

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.site_name
documentation: The title to use for the documentation.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.nav
documentation: Defines the structure of the navigation.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.exclude_docs
documentation: Gitignore-like patterns of files (relative to docs dir) to exclude from the site.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.draft_docs
documentation: Gitignore-like patterns of files (relative to docs dir) to mark as draft.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.not_in_nav
documentation:
Gitignore-like patterns of files (relative to docs dir) that are not intended to be in the nav.

This marks doc files that are expected not to be in the nav, otherwise they will cause a log message
(see also `validation.nav.omitted_files`).

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.site_url
documentation: The full URL to where the documentation will be hosted.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.site_description
documentation:
A description for the documentation project that will be added to the
HTML meta tags.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.site_author
documentation: The name of the author to add to the HTML meta tags.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.theme
documentation: The MkDocs theme for the documentation.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.docs_dir
documentation: The directory containing the documentation markdown.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.site_dir
documentation: The directory where the site will be built to

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.copyright
documentation: A copyright notice to add to the footer of documentation.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.google_analytics
documentation:
set of values for Google analytics containing the account IO and domain
this should look like, ['UA-27795084-5', 'mkdocs.org']

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.dev_addr
documentation: The address on which to serve the live reloading docs server.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.use_directory_urls
documentation:
If `True`, use `<page_name>/index.html` style files with hyperlinks to
the directory. If `False`, use `<page_name>.html style file with
hyperlinks to the file.
True generates nicer URLs, but False is useful if browsing the output on
a filesystem.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.repo_url
documentation:
Specify a link to the project source repo to be included
in the documentation pages.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.repo_name
documentation:
A name to use for the link to the project source repo.
Default, If repo_url is unset then None, otherwise
"GitHub", "Bitbucket" or "GitLab" for known url or Hostname
for unknown urls.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.edit_uri
documentation:
Specify a URI to the docs dir in the project source repo, relative to the
repo_url. When set, a link directly to the page in the source repo will
be added to the generated HTML. If repo_url is not set also, this option
is ignored.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.extra_javascript
documentation:
Specify which css or javascript files from the docs directory should be
additionally included in the site.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.extra_templates
documentation:
Similar to the above, but each template (HTML or XML) will be build with
Jinja2 and the global context.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.markdown_extensions
documentation: PyMarkdown extension names.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.mdx_configs
documentation: PyMarkdown extension configs. Populated from `markdown_extensions`.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.strict
documentation:
Enabling strict mode causes MkDocs to stop the build when a problem is
encountered rather than display an error.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.remote_branch
documentation: The remote branch to commit to when using gh-deploy.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.remote_name
documentation: The remote name to push to when using gh-deploy.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.extra
documentation:
extra is a mapping/dictionary of data that is passed to the template.
This allows template authors to require extra configuration that not
relevant to all themes and doesn't need to be explicitly supported by
MkDocs itself. A good example here would be including the current
project version.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.plugins
documentation:
A list of plugins. Each item may contain a string name or a key value pair.
A key value pair should be the string name (as the key) and a dict of config
options (as the value).

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.hooks
documentation:
A list of filenames that will be imported as Python modules and used as
an instance of a plugin each.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.watch
documentation: A list of extra paths to watch while running `mkdocs serve`.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig._current_page
documentation:
The currently rendered page. Please do not access this and instead
rely on the `page` argument to event handlers.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.Validation.NavValidation.omitted_files
documentation:
Warning level for when a doc file is never mentioned in the navigation.
For granular configuration, see `not_in_nav`.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.Validation.NavValidation.not_found
documentation: Warning level for when the navigation links to a relative path that isn't an existing page on the site.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.Validation.NavValidation.absolute_links
documentation: Warning level for when the navigation links to an absolute path (starting with `/`).

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.Validation.LinksValidation.not_found
documentation: Warning level for when a Markdown doc links to a relative path that isn't an existing document on the site.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.Validation.LinksValidation.absolute_links
documentation: Warning level for when a Markdown doc links to an absolute path (starting with `/`).

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.Validation.LinksValidation.unrecognized_links
documentation:
Warning level for when a Markdown doc links to a relative path that doesn't look like
it could be a valid internal link. For example, if the link ends with `/`.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.defaults.MkDocsConfig.Validation.LinksValidation.anchors
documentation: Warning level for when a Markdown doc links to an anchor that's not present on the target page.
The configuration of MkDocs itself (the root object of mkdocs.yml).
NOTE: The order here is important. During validation some config options
depend on others. So, if config option A depends on B, then A should be
listed higher in the schema.

modules.name: mkdocs
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
For acceptable version formats, see https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/

functions.name: mkdocs.config.base.get_schema
documentation: Extract ConfigOptions defined in a class (used just as a container) and put them into a schema tuple.

functions.name: mkdocs.config.base._open_config_file
documentation:
A context manager which yields an open file descriptor ready to be read.

Accepts a filename as a string, an open or closed file descriptor, or None.
When None, it defaults to `mkdocs.yml` in the CWD. If a closed file descriptor
is received, a new file descriptor is opened for the same file.

The file descriptor is automatically closed when the context manager block is existed.

functions.name: mkdocs.config.base.load_config
documentation:
Load the configuration for a given file object or name.

The config_file can either be a file object, string or None. If it is None
the default `mkdocs.yml` filename will loaded.

Extra kwargs are passed to the configuration to replace any default values
unless they themselves are None.

methods.name: mkdocs.config.base.BaseConfigOption.pre_validation
documentation:
Before all options are validated, perform a pre-validation process.

The pre-validation process method should be implemented by subclasses.

methods.name: mkdocs.config.base.BaseConfigOption.run_validation
documentation:
Perform validation for a value.

The run_validation method should be implemented by subclasses.

methods.name: mkdocs.config.base.BaseConfigOption.post_validation
documentation:
After all options have passed validation, perform a post-validation
process to do any additional changes dependent on other config values.

The post-validation process method should be implemented by subclasses.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.base.ValidationError
documentation: Raised during the validation process of the config on errors.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.base.Config

methods.name: mkdocs.config.base.Config.__new__
documentation: Compatibility: allow referring to `LegacyConfig(...)` constructor as `Config(...)`.

methods.name: mkdocs.config.base.Config.set_defaults
documentation:
Set the base config by going through each validator and getting the
default if it has one.

methods.name: mkdocs.config.base.Config.load_dict
documentation: Load config options from a dictionary.

methods.name: mkdocs.config.base.Config.load_file
documentation: Load config options from the open file descriptor of a YAML file.

Base class for MkDocs configuration, plugin configuration (and sub-configuration) objects.

It should be subclassed and have `ConfigOption`s defined as attributes.
For examples, see mkdocs/contrib/search/__init__.py and mkdocs/config/defaults.py.

Behavior as it was prior to MkDocs 1.4 is now handled by LegacyConfig.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.base.LegacyConfig
documentation: A configuration object for plugins, as just a dict without type-safe attribute access.

classes.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.LangOption
documentation: Validate Language(s) provided in config are known languages.

classes.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.SearchPlugin

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.SearchPlugin.on_config
documentation: Add plugin templates and scripts to config.

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.SearchPlugin.on_pre_build
documentation: Create search index instance for later use.

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.SearchPlugin.on_page_context
documentation: Add page to search index.

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.SearchPlugin.on_post_build
documentation: Build search index.
Add a search feature to MkDocs.

classes.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.SearchIndex

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.SearchIndex._find_toc_by_id
documentation:
Given a table of contents and HTML ID, iterate through
and return the matched item in the TOC.

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.SearchIndex._add_entry
documentation: A simple wrapper to add an entry, dropping bad characters.

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.SearchIndex.add_entry_from_context
documentation:
Create a set of entries in the index for a page. One for
the page itself and then one for each of its' heading
tags.

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.SearchIndex.create_entry_for_section
documentation:
Given a section on the page, the table of contents and
the absolute url for the page create an entry in the
index.

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.SearchIndex.generate_search_index
documentation: Python to json conversion.

Search index is a collection of pages and sections (heading
tags and their following content are sections).

classes.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.ContentSection
documentation:
Used by the ContentParser class to capture the information we
need when it is parsing the HTML.

classes.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.ContentParser

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.ContentParser.handle_starttag
documentation: Called at the start of every HTML tag.

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.ContentParser.handle_endtag
documentation: Called at the end of every HTML tag.

methods.name: mkdocs.contrib.search.search_index.ContentParser.handle_data
documentation: Called for the text contents of each tag.

Given a block of HTML, group the content under the preceding
heading tags which can then be used for creating an index
for that section.

functions.name: mkdocs.commands.serve.serve
documentation:
Start the MkDocs development server.

By default it will serve the documentation on http://localhost:8000/ and
it will rebuild the documentation and refresh the page automatically
whenever a file is edited.

functions.name: mkdocs.livereload._normalize_mount_path
documentation: Ensure the mount path starts and ends with a slash.

functions.name: mkdocs.livereload._try_relativize_path
documentation: Make the path relative to current directory if it's under that directory.

methods.name: mkdocs.livereload.LiveReloadServer.watch
documentation: Add the 'path' to watched paths, call the function and reload when any file changes under it.

methods.name: mkdocs.livereload.LiveReloadServer.unwatch
documentation: Stop watching file changes for path. Raises if there was no corresponding `watch` call.

functions.name: mkdocs.commands.build.get_context
documentation: Return the template context for a given page or template.

functions.name: mkdocs.commands.build._build_template
documentation: Return rendered output for given template as a string.

functions.name: mkdocs.commands.build._build_theme_template
documentation: Build a template using the theme environment.

functions.name: mkdocs.commands.build._build_extra_template
documentation: Build user templates which are not part of the theme.

functions.name: mkdocs.commands.build._populate_page
documentation: Read page content from docs_dir and render Markdown.

functions.name: mkdocs.commands.build._build_page
documentation: Pass a Page to theme template and write output to site_dir.

functions.name: mkdocs.commands.build.build
documentation: Perform a full site build.

functions.name: mkdocs.commands.build.site_directory_contains_stale_files
documentation: Check if the site directory contains stale files from a previous build.

modules.name: mkdocs.structure.toc
documentation:
Deals with generating the per-page table of contents.

For the sake of simplicity we use the Python-Markdown `toc` extension to
generate a list of dicts for each toc item, and then store it as AnchorLinks to
maintain compatibility with older versions of MkDocs.

classes.name: mkdocs.structure.toc.AnchorLink

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.toc.AnchorLink.url
documentation: The hash fragment of a URL pointing to the item.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.toc.AnchorLink.title
documentation: The text of the item, as HTML.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.toc.AnchorLink.level
documentation: The zero-based level of the item.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.toc.AnchorLink.children
documentation: An iterable of any child items.
A single entry in the table of contents.

classes.name: mkdocs.structure.toc.TableOfContents
documentation: Represents the table of contents for a given page.

classes.name: mkdocs.structure.StructureItem

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.StructureItem.parent
documentation: The immediate parent of the item in the site navigation. `None` if it's at the top level.
An item in MkDocs structure - see concrete subclasses Section, Page or Link.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.SubConfig

methods.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.SubConfig.__init__
documentation: Create a sub-config in a type-safe way, using fields defined in a Config subclass.

methods.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.SubConfig.__init__
documentation: Create an untyped sub-config, using directly passed fields.

methods.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.SubConfig.__class_getitem__
documentation: Eliminates the need to write `config_class = FooConfig` when subclassing SubConfig[FooConfig].

Subconfig Config Option.

New: If targeting MkDocs 1.4+, please pass a subclass of Config to the
constructor, instead of the old style of a sequence of ConfigOption instances.
Validation is then enabled by default.

A set of `config_options` grouped under a single config option.
By default, validation errors and warnings resulting from validating
`config_options` are ignored (`validate=False`). Users should typically
enable validation with `validate=True`.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.PropagatingSubConfig
documentation:
A SubConfig that must consist of SubConfigs with defined schemas.

Any value set on the top config gets moved to sub-configs with matching keys.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.OptionallyRequired

methods.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.OptionallyRequired.validate
documentation:
Perform some initial validation.

If the option is empty (None) and isn't required, leave it as such. If
it is empty but has a default, use that. Finally, call the
run_validation method on the subclass unless.

Soft-deprecated, do not use.

A subclass of BaseConfigOption that adds support for default values and
required values. It is a base class for config options.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.ListOfItems

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.ListOfItems.required
documentation: Only for subclasses to set.

Validates a homogeneous list of items.

E.g. for `config_options.ListOfItems(config_options.Type(int))` a valid item is `[1, 2, 3]`.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.DictOfItems

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.DictOfItems.required
documentation: Only for subclasses to set.

Validates a dict of items. Keys are always strings.

E.g. for `config_options.DictOfItems(config_options.Type(int))` a valid item is `{"a": 1, "b": 2}`.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.ConfigItems
documentation:
Deprecated: Use `ListOfItems(SubConfig(...))` instead of `ConfigItems(...)`.

Validates a list of mappings that all must match the same set of
options.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.Type
documentation:
Type Config Option.

Validate the type of a config option against a given Python type.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.Choice
documentation:
Choice Config Option.

Validate the config option against a strict set of values.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.Deprecated
documentation:
Deprecated Config Option.

Raises a warning as the option is deprecated. Uses `message` for the
warning. If `move_to` is set to the name of a new config option, the value
is moved to the new option on pre_validation. If `option_type` is set to a
ConfigOption instance, then the value is validated against that type.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.IpAddress
documentation:
IpAddress Config Option.

Validate that an IP address is in an appropriate format

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.URL
documentation:
URL Config Option.

Validate a URL by requiring a scheme is present.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.Optional
documentation:
Wraps a field and makes a None value possible for it when no value is set.

E.g. `my_field = config_options.Optional(config_options.Type(str))`

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.FilesystemObject
documentation: Base class for options that point to filesystem objects.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.Dir
documentation:
Dir Config Option.

Validate a path to a directory, optionally verifying that it exists.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.File
documentation:
File Config Option.

Validate a path to a file, optionally verifying that it exists.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.ListOfPaths
documentation:
List of Paths Config Option.

A list of file system paths. Raises an error if one of the paths does not exist.

For greater flexibility, prefer ListOfItems, e.g. to require files specifically:

    config_options.ListOfItems(config_options.File(exists=True))

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.SiteDir
documentation:
SiteDir Config Option.

Validates the site_dir and docs_dir directories do not contain each other.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.Theme
documentation:
Theme Config Option.

Validate that the theme exists and build Theme instance.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.Nav
documentation:
Nav Config Option.

Validate the Nav config.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.Private
documentation: A config option that can only be populated programmatically. Raises an error if set by the user.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.ExtraScriptValue

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.ExtraScriptValue.path
documentation: The value of the `src` tag of the script.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.ExtraScriptValue.type
documentation: The value of the `type` tag of the script.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.ExtraScriptValue.defer
documentation: Whether to add the `defer` tag to the script.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.ExtraScriptValue.async_
documentation: Whether to add the `async` tag to the script.
An extra script to be added to the page. The `extra_javascript` config is a list of these.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.MarkdownExtensions
documentation:
Markdown Extensions Config Option.

A list or dict of extensions. Each list item may contain either a string or a one item dict.
A string must be a valid Markdown extension name with no config options defined. The key of
a dict item must be a valid Markdown extension name and the value must be a dict of config
options for that extension. Extension configs are set on the private setting passed to
`configkey`. The `builtins` keyword accepts a list of extensions which cannot be overridden by
the user. However, builtins can be duplicated to define config options for them if desired.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.Plugins
documentation:
Plugins config option.

A list or dict of plugins. If a plugin defines config options those are used when
initializing the plugin class.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.Hooks
documentation: A list of Python scripts to be treated as instances of plugins.

classes.name: mkdocs.config.config_options.PathSpec
documentation: A path pattern based on gitignore-like syntax.

modules.name: mkdocs.__main__
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: mkdocs.__main__.cli
documentation: MkDocs - Project documentation with Markdown.

functions.name: mkdocs.__main__.serve_command
documentation: Run the builtin development server.

functions.name: mkdocs.__main__.build_command
documentation: Build the MkDocs documentation.

functions.name: mkdocs.__main__.gh_deploy_command
documentation: Deploy your documentation to GitHub Pages.

functions.name: mkdocs.__main__.get_deps_command
documentation: Show required PyPI packages inferred from plugins in mkdocs.yml.

functions.name: mkdocs.__main__.new_command
documentation: Create a new MkDocs project.

classes.name: mkdocs.__main__.State
documentation: Maintain logging level.

modules.name: mkdocs.utils.meta
documentation:

MultiMarkdown Meta-Data

Extracts, parses and transforms MultiMarkdown style data from documents.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.meta.get_data
documentation:
Extract meta-data from a text document.

Returns a tuple of document and a data dict.

functions.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.get_navigation
documentation: Build site navigation from config and files.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Navigation.homepage
documentation: The [page][mkdocs.structure.pages.Page] object for the homepage of the site.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Navigation.pages
documentation: A flat list of all [page][mkdocs.structure.pages.Page] objects contained in the navigation.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Section.active
documentation:
When `True`, indicates that a child page of this section is the current page and
can be used to highlight the section as the currently viewed section. Defaults
to `False`.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Section.active
documentation: Set active status of section and ancestors.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Section.title
documentation: The title of the section.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Section.children
documentation: An iterable of all child navigation objects. Children may include nested sections, pages and links.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Section.is_section
documentation: Indicates that the navigation object is a "section" object. Always `True` for section objects.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Section.is_page
documentation: Indicates that the navigation object is a "page" object. Always `False` for section objects.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Section.is_link
documentation: Indicates that the navigation object is a "link" object. Always `False` for section objects.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Link.title
documentation: The title of the link. This would generally be used as the label of the link.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Link.url
documentation:
The URL that the link points to. The URL should always be an absolute URLs and
should not need to have `base_url` prepended.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Link.children
documentation: Links do not contain children and the attribute is always `None`.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Link.active
documentation: External links cannot be "active" and the attribute is always `False`.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Link.is_section
documentation: Indicates that the navigation object is a "section" object. Always `False` for link objects.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Link.is_page
documentation: Indicates that the navigation object is a "page" object. Always `False` for link objects.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.nav.Link.is_link
documentation: Indicates that the navigation object is a "link" object. Always `True` for link objects.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.cache.download_and_cache_url
documentation:
Downloads a file from the URL, stores it under ~/.cache/, and returns its content.

For tracking the age of the content, a prefix is inserted into the stored file, rather than relying on mtime.

Args:
    url: URL to use.
    download: Callback that will accept the URL and actually perform the download.
    cache_duration: How long to consider the URL content cached.
    comment: The appropriate comment prefix for this file format.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.rendering.get_heading_text
documentation: TODO: Most of this file will become unnecessary after https://github.com/Python-Markdown/markdown/pull/1441

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.rendering._strip_tags
documentation: Strip HTML tags and return plain text. Note: HTML entities are unaffected.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.rendering._remove_anchorlink
documentation: Drop anchorlink from the element, if present.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.rendering._remove_fnrefs
documentation: Remove footnote references from the element, if any are present.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.rendering._extract_alt_texts
documentation: For images that have an `alt` attribute, replace them with this content.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.rendering._replace_elements_with_text
documentation: For each child element, if matched, replace it with the text returned from the predicate.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.url
documentation: The URL of the page relative to the MkDocs `site_dir`.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.active
documentation: When `True`, indicates that this page is the currently viewed page. Defaults to `False`.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.active
documentation: Set active status of page and ancestors.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.is_homepage
documentation: Evaluates to `True` for the homepage of the site and `False` for all other pages.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.title
documentation:
Returns the title for the current page.

Before calling `read_source()`, this value is empty. It can also be updated by `render()`.

Checks these in order and uses the first that returns a valid title:

- value provided on init (passed in from config)
- value of metadata 'title'
- content of the first H1 in Markdown content
- convert filename to title

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.render
documentation: Convert the Markdown source file to HTML as per the config.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.markdown
documentation: The original Markdown content from the file.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.content
documentation:
The rendered Markdown as HTML, this is the contents of the documentation.

Populated after `.render()`.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.toc
documentation:
An iterable object representing the Table of contents for a page. Each item in
the `toc` is an [`AnchorLink`][mkdocs.structure.toc.AnchorLink].

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.meta
documentation: A mapping of the metadata included at the top of the markdown page.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.file
documentation: The documentation [`File`][mkdocs.structure.files.File] that the page is being rendered from.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.abs_url
documentation:
The absolute URL of the page from the server root as determined by the value
assigned to the [site_url][] configuration setting. The value includes any
subdirectory included in the `site_url`, but not the domain. [base_url][] should
not be used with this variable.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.canonical_url
documentation:
The full, canonical URL to the current page as determined by the value assigned
to the [site_url][] configuration setting. The value includes the domain and any
subdirectory included in the `site_url`. [base_url][] should not be used with this
variable.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.edit_url
documentation:
The full URL to the source page in the source repository. Typically used to
provide a link to edit the source page. [base_url][] should not be used with this
variable.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.previous_page
documentation:
The [page][mkdocs.structure.pages.Page] object for the previous page or `None`.
The value will be `None` if the current page is the first item in the site navigation
or if the current page is not included in the navigation at all.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.next_page
documentation:
The [page][mkdocs.structure.pages.Page] object for the next page or `None`.
The value will be `None` if the current page is the last item in the site navigation
or if the current page is not included in the navigation at all.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.children
documentation: Pages do not contain children and the attribute is always `None`.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.is_section
documentation: Indicates that the navigation object is a "section" object. Always `False` for page objects.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.is_page
documentation: Indicates that the navigation object is a "page" object. Always `True` for page objects.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.is_link
documentation: Indicates that the navigation object is a "link" object. Always `False` for page objects.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.present_anchor_ids
documentation: Anchor IDs that this page contains (can be linked to in this page).

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.pages.Page.links_to_anchors
documentation:
Links to anchors in other files that this page contains.

The structure is: `{file_that_is_linked_to: {'anchor': 'original_link/to/some_file.md#anchor'}}`.
Populated after `.render()`. Populated only if `validation: {anchors: info}` (or greater) is set.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.pages._RelativePathTreeprocessor.run
documentation:
Update urls on anchors and images to make them relative.

Iterates through the full document tree looking for specific
tags and then makes them relative based on the site navigation

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.pages._RelativePathTreeprocessor._possible_target_uris
documentation: First yields the resolved file uri for the link, then proceeds to yield guesses for possible mistakes.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.templates.url_filter

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.templates.script_tag_filter

class_variables.name: mkdocs.utils.templates.TemplateContext.extra_css

class_variables.name: mkdocs.utils.templates.TemplateContext.extra_javascript
documentation: Do not use, prefer `config.extra_javascript`.

modules.name: mkdocs.utils
documentation:
Standalone file utils.

Nothing in this module should have an knowledge of config or the layout
and structure of the site and pages in the site.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.get_build_timestamp
documentation:
Returns the number of seconds since the epoch for the latest updated page.

In reality this is just today's date because that's how pages' update time is populated.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.get_build_datetime
documentation:
Returns an aware datetime object.

Support SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH environment variable for reproducible builds.
See https://reproducible-builds.org/specs/source-date-epoch/

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.get_build_date
documentation:
Returns the displayable date string.

Support SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH environment variable for reproducible builds.
See https://reproducible-builds.org/specs/source-date-epoch/

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.reduce_list
documentation: Reduce duplicate items in a list and preserve order.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.copy_file
documentation:
Copy source_path to output_path, making sure any parent directories exist.

The output_path may be a directory.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.write_file
documentation: Write content to output_path, making sure any parent directories exist.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.clean_directory
documentation: Remove the content of a directory recursively but not the directory itself.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.is_markdown_file
documentation:
Return True if the given file path is a Markdown file.

https://superuser.com/questions/249436/file-extension-for-markdown-files

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.is_error_template
documentation: Return True if the given file path is an HTTP error template.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.get_relative_url
documentation:
Return given url relative to other.

Both are operated as slash-separated paths, similarly to the 'path' part of a URL.
The last component of `other` is skipped if it contains a dot (considered a file).
Actual URLs (with schemas etc.) aren't supported. The leading slash is ignored.
Paths are normalized ('..' works as parent directory), but going higher than the
root has no effect ('foo/../../bar' ends up just as 'bar').

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.normalize_url
documentation: Return a URL relative to the given page or using the base.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.create_media_urls
documentation: Soft-deprecated, do not use.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.get_theme_dir
documentation: Return the directory of an installed theme by name.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.get_themes

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.get_theme_names

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.dirname_to_title

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.get_markdown_title

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.find_or_create_node

Given a list, look for dictionary with a key matching key and return it's
value. If it doesn't exist, create it with the value of an empty list and
return that.

functions.name: mkdocs.utils.nest_paths

Given a list of paths, convert them into a nested structure that will match

methods.name: mkdocs.utils.yaml._DirPlaceholder.__fspath__
documentation: Can be used as a path.

methods.name: mkdocs.utils.yaml._DirPlaceholder.__str__
documentation: Can be converted to a string to obtain the current class.

classes.name: mkdocs.utils.yaml.ConfigDirPlaceholder
documentation:
A placeholder object that gets resolved to the directory of the config file when used as a path.

The suffix can be an additional sub-path that is always appended to this path.

This is the implementation of the `!relative $config_dir/suffix` tag, but can also be passed programmatically.

classes.name: mkdocs.utils.yaml.DocsDirPlaceholder
documentation:
A placeholder object that gets resolved to the docs dir when used as a path.

The suffix can be an additional sub-path that is always appended to this path.

This is the implementation of the `!relative $docs_dir/suffix` tag, but can also be passed programmatically.

classes.name: mkdocs.utils.yaml.RelativeDirPlaceholder
documentation:
A placeholder object that gets resolved to the directory of the Markdown file currently being rendered.

This is the implementation of the `!relative` tag, but can also be passed programmatically.

functions.name: mkdocs.structure.files.set_exclusions
documentation: Re-calculate which files are excluded, based on the patterns in the config.

functions.name: mkdocs.structure.files.file_sort_key
documentation:
Replicates the sort order how `get_files` produces it - index first, directories last.

To sort a list of `File`, pass as the `key` argument to `sort`.

functions.name: mkdocs.structure.files._file_sort_key
documentation: Always sort `index` or `README` as first filename in list. This works only on basenames of files.

functions.name: mkdocs.structure.files._sort_files

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.InclusionLevel.EXCLUDED
documentation: The file is excluded and will not be processed.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.InclusionLevel.DRAFT
documentation: The file is excluded from the final site, but will still be populated during `mkdocs serve`.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.InclusionLevel.NOT_IN_NAV
documentation: The file is part of the site, but doesn't produce nav warnings.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.InclusionLevel.UNDEFINED
documentation: Still needs to be computed based on the config. If the config doesn't kick in, acts the same as `included`.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.InclusionLevel.INCLUDED
documentation: The file is part of the site. Documentation pages that are omitted from the nav will produce warnings.

classes.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.src_path
documentation: Same as `src_uri` (and synchronized with it) but will use backslashes on Windows. Discouraged.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.dest_path
documentation: Same as `dest_uri` (and synchronized with it) but will use backslashes on Windows. Discouraged.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.generated
documentation:
Create a virtual file backed by in-memory content.

It will pretend to be a file in the docs dir at `src_uri`.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.generated
documentation:
Create a virtual file backed by a physical temporary file at `abs_src_path`.

It will pretend to be a file in the docs dir at `src_uri`.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.generated
documentation:
Create a virtual file, backed either by in-memory `content` or by a file at `abs_src_path`.

It will pretend to be a file in the docs dir at `src_uri`.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.edit_uri
documentation:
A path relative to the source repository to use for the "edit" button.

Defaults to `src_uri` and can be overwritten.
For generated files this should be set to `None`.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File._get_stem
documentation: Soft-deprecated, do not use.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File._get_dest_path
documentation: Soft-deprecated, do not use.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File._get_url
documentation: Soft-deprecated, do not use.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.abs_src_path
documentation:
The absolute concrete path of the source file. Will use backslashes on Windows.

Note: do not use this path to read the file, prefer `content_bytes`/`content_string`.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.abs_dest_path
documentation: The absolute concrete path of the destination file. Will use backslashes on Windows.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.url_relative_to
documentation: Return url for file relative to other file.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.content_bytes
documentation:
Get the content of this file as a bytestring.

May raise if backed by a real file (`abs_src_path`) if it cannot be read.

If used as a setter, it defines the content of the file, and `abs_src_path` becomes unset.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.content_string
documentation:
Get the content of this file as a string. Assumes UTF-8 encoding, may raise.

May also raise if backed by a real file (`abs_src_path`) if it cannot be read.

If used as a setter, it defines the content of the file, and `abs_src_path` becomes unset.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.copy_file
documentation: Copy source file to destination, ensuring parent directories exist.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.is_documentation_page
documentation: Return True if file is a Markdown page.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.is_static_page
documentation: Return True if file is a static page (HTML, XML, JSON).

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.is_media_file
documentation: Return True if file is not a documentation or static page.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.is_javascript
documentation: Return True if file is a JavaScript file.

methods.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.is_css
documentation: Return True if file is a CSS file.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.src_uri
documentation: The pure path (always '/'-separated) of the source file relative to the source directory.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.use_directory_urls
documentation:
Whether directory URLs ('foo/') should be used or not ('foo.html').

If `False`, a Markdown file is mapped to an HTML file of the same name (the file extension is
changed to `.html`). If True, a Markdown file is mapped to an HTML index file (`index.html`)
nested in a directory using the "name" of the file in `path`. Non-Markdown files retain their
original path.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.src_dir
documentation:
The OS path of the top-level directory that the source file originates from.

Assumed to be the *docs_dir*; not populated for generated files.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.dest_dir
documentation: The OS path of the destination directory (top-level site_dir) that the file should be copied to.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.inclusion
documentation: Whether the file will be excluded from the built site.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.generated_by
documentation:
If not None, indicates that a plugin generated this file on the fly.

The value is the plugin's entrypoint name and can be used to find the plugin by key in the PluginCollection.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File._content
documentation:
If set, the file's content will be read from here.

This logic is handled by `content_bytes`/`content_string`, which should be used instead of
accessing this attribute.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.name
documentation: Return the name of the file without its extension.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.dest_uri
documentation: The pure path (always '/'-separated) of the destination file relative to the destination directory.

class_variables.name: mkdocs.structure.files.File.url
documentation: The URI of the destination file relative to the destination directory as a string.

A MkDocs File object.

It represents how the contents of one file should be populated in the destination site.

A file always has its `abs_dest_path` (obtained by joining `dest_dir` and `dest_path`),
where the `dest_dir` is understood to be the *site* directory.

`content_bytes`/`content_string` (new in MkDocs 1.6) can always be used to obtain the file's
content. But it may be backed by one of the two sources:

*   A physical source file at `abs_src_path` (by default obtained by joining `src_dir` and
    `src_uri`). `src_dir` is understood to be the *docs* directory.

    Then `content_bytes`/`content_string` will read the file at `abs_src_path`.

    `src_dir` *should* be populated for real files and should be `None` for generated files.

*   Since MkDocs 1.6 a file may alternatively be stored in memory - `content_string`/`content_bytes`.

    Then `src_dir` and `abs_src_path` will remain `None`. `content_bytes`/`content_string` need
    to be written to, or populated through the `content` argument in the constructor.

    But `src_uri` is still populated for such files as well! The virtual file pretends as if it
    originated from that path in the `docs` directory, and other values are derived.

For static files the file is just copied to the destination, and `dest_uri` equals `src_uri`.

For Markdown files (determined by the file extension in `src_uri`) the destination content
will be the rendered content, and `dest_uri` will have the `.html` extension and some
additional transformations to the path, based on `use_directory_urls`.

modules.name: mkdocs.plugins
documentation: Implements the plugin API for MkDocs.

functions.name: mkdocs.plugins.get_plugins
documentation: Return a dict of all installed Plugins as {name: EntryPoint}.

functions.name: mkdocs.plugins.event_priority
documentation:
A decorator to set an event priority for an event handler method.

Recommended priority values:
`100` "first", `50` "early", `0` "default", `-50` "late", `-100` "last".
As different plugins discover more precise relations to each other, the values should be further tweaked.

Usage example:

```python
@plugins.event_priority(-100)  # Wishing to run this after all other plugins' `on_files` events.
def on_files(self, files, config, **kwargs):
    ...
```

New in MkDocs 1.4.
Recommended shim for backwards compatibility:

```python
try:
    from mkdocs.plugins import event_priority
except ImportError:
    event_priority = lambda priority: lambda f: f  # No-op fallback
```

functions.name: mkdocs.plugins.get_plugin_logger
documentation:
Return a logger for plugins.

Arguments:
    name: The name to use with `logging.getLogger`.

Returns:
    A logger configured to work well in MkDocs,
        prefixing each message with the plugin package name.

Example:
    ```python
    from mkdocs.plugins import get_plugin_logger

    log = get_plugin_logger(__name__)
    log.info("My plugin message")
    ```

classes.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.__class_getitem__
documentation: Eliminates the need to write `config_class = FooConfig` when subclassing BasePlugin[FooConfig].

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.load_config
documentation: Load config from a dict of options. Returns a tuple of (errors, warnings).

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_startup
documentation:
The `startup` event runs once at the very beginning of an `mkdocs` invocation.

New in MkDocs 1.4.

The presence of an `on_startup` method (even if empty) migrates the plugin to the new
system where the plugin object is kept across builds within one `mkdocs serve`.

Note that for initializing variables, the `__init__` method is still preferred.
For initializing per-build variables (and whenever in doubt), use the `on_config` event.

Args:
    command: the command that MkDocs was invoked with, e.g. "serve" for `mkdocs serve`.
    dirty: whether `--dirty` flag was passed.
One-time events

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_shutdown
documentation:
The `shutdown` event runs once at the very end of an `mkdocs` invocation, before exiting.

This event is relevant only for support of `mkdocs serve`, otherwise within a
single build it's undistinguishable from `on_post_build`.

New in MkDocs 1.4.

The presence of an `on_shutdown` method (even if empty) migrates the plugin to the new
system where the plugin object is kept across builds within one `mkdocs serve`.

Note the `on_post_build` method is still preferred for cleanups, when possible, as it has
a much higher chance of actually triggering. `on_shutdown` is "best effort" because it
relies on detecting a graceful shutdown of MkDocs.

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_serve
documentation:
The `serve` event is only called when the `serve` command is used during
development. It runs only once, after the first build finishes.
It is passed the `Server` instance which can be modified before
it is activated. For example, additional files or directories could be added
to the list of "watched" files for auto-reloading.

Args:
    server: `livereload.Server` instance
    config: global configuration object
    builder: a callable which gets passed to each call to `server.watch`

Returns:
    `livereload.Server` instance

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_config
documentation:
The `config` event is the first event called on build and is run immediately
after the user configuration is loaded and validated. Any alterations to the
config should be made here.

Args:
    config: global configuration object

Returns:
    global configuration object
Global events

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_pre_build
documentation:
The `pre_build` event does not alter any variables. Use this event to call
pre-build scripts.

Args:
    config: global configuration object

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_files
documentation:
The `files` event is called after the files collection is populated from the
`docs_dir`. Use this event to add, remove, or alter files in the
collection. Note that Page objects have not yet been associated with the
file objects in the collection. Use [Page Events](plugins.md#page-events) to manipulate page
specific data.

Args:
    files: global files collection
    config: global configuration object

Returns:
    global files collection

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_nav
documentation:
The `nav` event is called after the site navigation is created and can
be used to alter the site navigation.

Args:
    nav: global navigation object
    config: global configuration object
    files: global files collection

Returns:
    global navigation object

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_env
documentation:
The `env` event is called after the Jinja template environment is created
and can be used to alter the
[Jinja environment](https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/en/latest/api/#jinja2.Environment).

Args:
    env: global Jinja environment
    config: global configuration object
    files: global files collection

Returns:
    global Jinja Environment

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_post_build
documentation:
The `post_build` event does not alter any variables. Use this event to call
post-build scripts.

Args:
    config: global configuration object

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_build_error
documentation:
The `build_error` event is called after an exception of any kind
is caught by MkDocs during the build process.
Use this event to clean things up before MkDocs terminates. Note that any other
events which were scheduled to run after the error will have been skipped. See
[Handling Errors](plugins.md#handling-errors) for more details.

Args:
    error: exception raised

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_pre_template
documentation:
The `pre_template` event is called immediately after the subject template is
loaded and can be used to alter the template.

Args:
    template: a Jinja2 [Template](https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/en/latest/api/#jinja2.Template) object
    template_name: string filename of template
    config: global configuration object

Returns:
    a Jinja2 [Template](https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/en/latest/api/#jinja2.Template) object
Template events

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_template_context
documentation:
The `template_context` event is called immediately after the context is created
for the subject template and can be used to alter the context for that specific
template only.

Args:
    context: dict of template context variables
    template_name: string filename of template
    config: global configuration object

Returns:
    dict of template context variables

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_post_template
documentation:
The `post_template` event is called after the template is rendered, but before
it is written to disc and can be used to alter the output of the template.
If an empty string is returned, the template is skipped and nothing is is
written to disc.

Args:
    output_content: output of rendered template as string
    template_name: string filename of template
    config: global configuration object

Returns:
    output of rendered template as string

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_pre_page
documentation:
The `pre_page` event is called before any actions are taken on the subject
page and can be used to alter the `Page` instance.

Args:
    page: `mkdocs.structure.pages.Page` instance
    config: global configuration object
    files: global files collection

Returns:
    `mkdocs.structure.pages.Page` instance
Page events

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_page_read_source
documentation:
> DEPRECATED: Instead of this event, prefer one of these alternatives:
>
> * Since MkDocs 1.6, instead set `content_bytes`/`content_string` of a `File` inside [`on_files`][].
> * Usually (although it's not an exact alternative), `on_page_markdown` can serve the same purpose.

The `on_page_read_source` event can replace the default mechanism to read
the contents of a page's source from the filesystem.

Args:
    page: `mkdocs.structure.pages.Page` instance
    config: global configuration object

Returns:
    The raw source for a page as unicode string. If `None` is returned, the
        default loading from a file will be performed.

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_page_markdown
documentation:
The `page_markdown` event is called after the page's markdown is loaded
from file and can be used to alter the Markdown source text. The meta-
data has been stripped off and is available as `page.meta` at this point.

Args:
    markdown: Markdown source text of page as string
    page: `mkdocs.structure.pages.Page` instance
    config: global configuration object
    files: global files collection

Returns:
    Markdown source text of page as string

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_page_content
documentation:
The `page_content` event is called after the Markdown text is rendered to
HTML (but before being passed to a template) and can be used to alter the
HTML body of the page.

Args:
    html: HTML rendered from Markdown source as string
    page: `mkdocs.structure.pages.Page` instance
    config: global configuration object
    files: global files collection

Returns:
    HTML rendered from Markdown source as string

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_page_context
documentation:
The `page_context` event is called after the context for a page is created
and can be used to alter the context for that specific page only.

Args:
    context: dict of template context variables
    page: `mkdocs.structure.pages.Page` instance
    config: global configuration object
    nav: global navigation object

Returns:
    dict of template context variables

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.on_post_page
documentation:
The `post_page` event is called after the template is rendered, but
before it is written to disc and can be used to alter the output of the
page. If an empty string is returned, the page is skipped and nothing is
written to disc.

Args:
    output: output of rendered template as string
    page: `mkdocs.structure.pages.Page` instance
    config: global configuration object

Returns:
    output of rendered template as string

class_variables.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.config_class
documentation: type: ignore[assignment]

class_variables.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.config
documentation: type: ignore[assignment]

class_variables.name: mkdocs.plugins.BasePlugin.supports_multiple_instances
documentation: Set to true in subclasses to declare support for adding the same plugin multiple times.

Plugin base class.

All plugins should subclass this class.

classes.name: mkdocs.plugins.CombinedEvent

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.CombinedEvent.__call__
documentation: This is only for mypy, so CombinedEvent can be a valid override of the methods in BasePlugin

classes.name: mkdocs.plugins.PluginCollection

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.PluginCollection._register_event
documentation: Register a method for an event.

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.PluginCollection.run_event
documentation:
Run all registered methods of an event.

`item` is the object to be modified or replaced and returned by the event method.
If it isn't given the event method creates a new object to be returned.
All other keywords are variables for context, but would not generally
be modified by the event method.

A collection of plugins.

In addition to being a dict of Plugin instances, each event method is registered
upon being added. All registered methods for a given event can then be run in order
by calling `run_event`.

classes.name: mkdocs.plugins.PrefixedLogger

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.PrefixedLogger.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the logger adapter.

Arguments:
    prefix: The string to insert in front of every message.
    logger: The logger instance.

methods.name: mkdocs.plugins.PrefixedLogger.process
documentation:
Process the message.

Arguments:
    msg: The message:
    kwargs: Remaining arguments.

Returns:
    The processed message.
A logger adapter to prefix log messages.
